pi_items.source: setup_task.sh explicitly assigns the Analyst Role as 'Aerospace Meteorologist' in the mandate.
Task description and setup_task.sh both refer to 'Commercial Crew' missions and NASA operations.
setup_task.sh script assigns these precise file paths to PRATE_FILE and SST_FILE and ensures they exist before running the task.
setup_task.sh creates this exact directory to store the expected output deliverables.
setup_task.sh explicitly removes these specific expected output files before the task begins.

Python data extraction confirms Alpha SST (~28.6 C) is above the cyclone risk threshold (26.5 C), while Bravo SST (~20.2 C) is below it. Precipitation at Bravo is also significantly lower. Thus Bravo is definitively the safer site.
Executing a Python script extracting September Sea Surface Temperature from NOAA sst.ltm.1991-2020.nc at Alpha coordinates (29N, 87W / 273E) yields ~28.6 C, securely within the expected range.
Executing a Python script extracting September Sea Surface Temperature from NOAA sst.ltm.1991-2020.nc at Bravo coordinates (30N, 118W / 242E) yields ~20.2 C, securely within the expected range.

privileged_info_summary: The task relies on real-world NOAA OISST v2 and NCEP Reanalysis climatology datasets for September. Data extraction via Python confirms that Site Alpha (29N, 87W) has an SST of approximately 28.6 C and higher precipitation, exceeding the 26.5 C threshold for tropical cyclone risk. Site Bravo (30N, 118W) has an SST of approximately 20.2 C with negligible precipitation, making it definitively the colder, drier, and safer site for a Commercial Crew splashdown. These empirical values correspond directly with the metadata claims regarding SST ranges and correct site selection.
